perturbation.original_text: This Nondisclosure Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into by and between The National Archives, ( the "Disclosing Party "), and _______________ ________ , located at __________________ ________________________________________________ _ (the "Receiving Party "), for the purp ose of preventing the unauthoris ed disclosure of Sensitive Information as defined below , in line with HMG’s Security Policy Framework .
perturbation.changed_text: This Nondisclosure Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into by and between The National Archives, ( the "Disclosing Party "), and _______________ ________ , located at __________________ ________________________________________________ _ (the "Data Recipient"), for the purp ose of preventing the unauthoris ed disclosure of Sensitive Information as defined below , in line with HMG’s Security Policy Framework .
perturbation.explanation: The term "Receiving Party" is changed to "Data Recipient." This introduces ambiguity as the document consistently refers to "Receiving Party" throughout. It creates confusion, as it is not clearly defined whether 'Data Recipient' and 'Receiving Party' are the same entity, creating uncertainty in enforcement of obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Receiving Party" with "Data Recipient". The contradicted text repeatedly refers to the "Receiving Party" and their obligations. Because the contract now uses two different terms interchangeably without defining them, it's unclear whether the obligations assigned to the "Receiving Party" apply to the "Data Recipient", creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology' and making enforcement of obligations uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, " Sensitive Information" s hall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged might lead to the security of the Disclosing Party’s physical or information assets or the safety of its staff and customers being compromised .
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, " Sensitive Information" s hall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged . However, for the purposes of Data Recipient ONLY, Sensitive Information only includes staff personal information.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Sensitive Information" is altered. The original definition is broad, covering commercial value and security. The changed definition restricts "Sensitive Information" to only include staff personal information specifically for the Data Recipient (which was originally the Recipient Party). This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about what constitutes "Sensitive Information" for the purposes of the entire agreement and introduces potential legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Sensitive Information' for the Data Recipient, restricting it to only staff personal information. This contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines obligations for the 'Receiving Party' (now the Data Recipient) to protect and restrict access to Sensitive Information based on a broader definition that includes commercial value and security. This creates a direct conflict because the Data Recipient's obligations are now unclear – are they only responsible for staff personal information, or are they still bound by the original broader definition for other purposes outlined in the contradicted section?

perturbation.original_text: If Sensitive Information is in a physical or electronic form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word s OFFICIAL -SENSITIVE , or some similar warning , in line with HMG’s protective marking scheme.
perturbation.changed_text: If Confidential Data is in a physical or electronic form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word s OFFICIAL -SENSITIVE , or some similar warning , in line with HMG’s protective marking scheme.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Sensitive Information" is replaced with "Confidential Data" in the labeling requirement. This creates confusion as to what should be labeled. If "Sensitive Information" and "Confidential Data" are not the same, it will contradict the requirement that ALL "Sensitive Information" must be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires "Sensitive Information" to be labeled, but the changed text only requires "Confidential Data" to be labeled. The contradicted text discusses the Receiving Party's obligations regarding "Sensitive Information". If "Sensitive Information" and "Confidential Data" are not the same, then not all "Sensitive Information" will be labeled, violating the original intent of protecting all "Sensitive Information". This directly contradicts the Receiving Party's obligations to maintain and protect 'Sensitive Information' as defined in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement do not extend to information that is: (a) publicly known at the time of disclosure or subsequently be comes publicly known through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) discovered or created by the Receiving Party before disclosure by Disclosing Party; (c) learned by the Receiving Party through legitimate means other than from the Disclosing Party or Disclo sing Party's representatives; (d) is disclosed by Receiving Party with Disclosing Party's prior writ ten approval; or (e) independently developed by the Receiving Party outside the scope of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Data Recipient's obligations under this Agreement extend to all information, including that which is (a) publicly known at the time of disclosure or subsequently becomes publicly known through no fault of the Data Recipient; (b) discovered or created by the Data Recipient before disclosure by Disclosing Party; (c) learned by the Data Recipient through legitimate means other than from the Disclosing Party or Disclosing Party's representatives; (d) is disclosed by Data Recipient with Disclosing Party's prior written approval; or (e) independently developed by the Data Recipient outside the scope of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The clause regarding exclusions from 'Receiving Party's' obligations is changed to state that 'Data Recipient's' obligations extend to *all* information, including what was previously excluded. Considering the 'Receiving Party' and 'Data Recipient' might be considered the same in some context, this creates an in-text contradiction because it nullifies the listed exceptions to confidentiality, placing an absolute obligation on the Data Recipient. This contradicts the general exceptions provided in the original agreement for the Receiving Party, thus creating uncertainty and legal challenges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the 'Data Recipient's obligations under this Agreement extend to all information', including information that was previously excluded in the original text for 'Receiving Party'. The contradicted text outlines specific obligations of the 'Receiving Party' regarding confidential information. The explanation provided states the 'Receiving Party' and 'Data Recipient' might be considered the same in some context, thus creating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty within the agreement regarding the confidentiality obligations. Therefore, the change directly contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Data Handler shall allow unrestricted access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors, and third parties without any need for non-disclosure agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Receiving Party" implied obligations are directly contradicted by referring to 'Data Handler' and explicitly stating there are no restrictions and non-disclosure agreements are not needed. This creates a direct conflict of obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the 'Receiving Party' must restrict access to Sensitive Information and require non-disclosure agreements. The changed text, however, states that the 'Data Handler' (which, according to the explanation, refers to the same entity as 'Receiving Party') shall allow unrestricted access to Sensitive Information without non-disclosure agreements. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the 'Receiving Party' to maintain the information in strict confidence, restrict access, and require non-disclosure agreements. This is a clear conflict of obligation, as the changed text removes the restrictions and confidentiality requirements outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The non -disclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Sensitive Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Sensitive Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or otherwise needing protection until Disclosing Party send s Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whicheve r occurs first.
perturbation.changed_text: The data protection provisions of this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the termination of this Agreement and Data Recipient's duty to hold Sensitive Information in confidence shall cease immediately, regardless of whether the Sensitive Information qualifies as a trade secret or not.
perturbation.explanation: The long-term confidentiality obligation is altered. Initially, 'Receiving Party's' confidentiality duty continues even after the agreement terminates. In contrast, it now specifies that the 'Data Recipient's' duty ends immediately when the agreement is terminated. This introduces conflicting terms ('non-disclosure provisions' vs 'data protection provisions'), therefore, if 'Data Recipient' and 'Receiving Party' are deemed the same entity, it directly contradicts a core element of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The modified text explicitly states that the 'Data Recipient's' duty to maintain confidentiality ends immediately upon termination, while the contradicted text specifies that the 'Receiving Party' shall maintain confidentiality even after termination. The perturbation explanation highlights that 'Receiving Party' and 'Data Recipient' refer to the same entity, creating a direct conflict regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. This contradiction fits the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as it uses multiple terms interchangeably, leading to conflicting obligations.
